title: Lecture: Water Scarcity

segments.text: Water scarcity affects more than two billion people around the world.
It has two main forms: physical scarcity, where simply not enough water exists, and economic scarcity, where water exists but infrastructure is inadequate.
Solutions include better irrigation, desalination, water recycling, and most importantly, changes in agricultural practices.
Around seventy percent of all freshwater is used for farming, so even small efficiency improvements can have a major impact.

transcript: Water scarcity affects more than two billion people around the world. It has two main forms: physical scarcity, where simply not enough water exists, and economic scarcity, where water exists but infrastructure is inadequate. Solutions include better irrigation, desalination, water recycling, and most importantly, changes in agricultural practices. Around seventy percent of all freshwater is used for farming, so even small efficiency improvements can have a major impact.
